title: Citation Scanline
prompt:
Create a production-ready Citation Scanline text effect using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original editorial pattern displays a short statement divided into three selectable clauses. Selecting a clause moves a lime scanline under its text and updates a plain-language citation note such as Source 02: decision memo. The visual should feel like careful reading rather than decoration. Use a light Bento card with white and #F4F5F6 surfaces, #ECECEE border, 22px rounding, soft neutral shadow, #111111 text, and #4A4A4F supporting copy. Lime is the only bright accent and no card gradient is allowed.

Use a labelled group of native buttons, aria-pressed for the selected clause, and a polite live region for the current citation. Support click, Enter, and Space with at least 44px hit targets, a visible 3px focus ring, and logical tab order. Move the scanline with a spring at stiffness 380 and damping 28; fade the citation note over 150ms ease-out. Use only local deterministic state—no timers, random values, storage, remote content, or network requests—and prefer transforms and opacity.

Keep lines readable and wrapping safely at 320px; expand the line length moderately in a larger preview. Ensure AA contrast and do not rely on the lime line alone because the selected clause must also be named in text. Under prefers-reduced-motion, snap the line into place with a brief opacity update. Return complete client code, pure CSS approximation, and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML:

*/ .citation-scanline{max-width:340px;border-bottom:1px solid #ececee}.citation-scanline button{min-height:44px;border:0;border-bottom:4px solid transparent;background:#fff;color:#111;font-size:18px;font-weight:900}.citation-scanline button[aria-pressed=true]{border-bottom-color:#b7df38}.citation-scanline button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.citation-scanline *{transition:none}}
